title: Bridge Essay: Inner speech and the neurobiology of psychosis.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mantra as cognitive technology — predictive processing of language, inner speech as self-modeling.

**Inner speech and the neurobiology of psychosis.**. Neuroscience and biobehavioral reviews.. PMID: 42331125. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2026.106805. Aberrations of inner speech have been linked to psychotic symptoms such as thought insertion and auditory verbal hallucinations. These symptoms may reflect failures of prediction and source monitoring. Normally, efference copies of speech motor commands are sent to auditory cortices and suppressed, helping distinguish self-generated from external input.
